setup_gns¶

Usage¶

cg_spica setup_gns [-Go] <topfile 1> <nmol 1> [<topfile 2> <nmol 2> ... <topfile n> <nmol n>]
                   <paramfile> <coordfile>

Description¶

setup_gns generates simulation input files for CG-MD with SPICA using GENESIS. This program requires the following files as inputs:

  • topfile: SPICA topology file, distributed at SPICA web

  • paramfile: SPICA parameter file, distributed at SPICA web

  • coordfile: CG configuration file in PDB format

The topfile can be generated with json2top, ENM, or Go commands (for proteins). For more information on these, see json2top, ENM, and Go. The nmol is the number of topfile molecules included in your system. It is sensitive to the order of arguments, so the molecule topology and number should be given in the same order as given in the prepared configuration file in the PDB file.

Running the program with the appropriate input files yields the following outputs:

  • toppar/top_spica_charmm.rtf: CHARMM-style RTF (residue topology file) for GENESIS

  • toppar/par_spica_charmm.prm: CHARMM-style parameter file for GENESIS

  • gns_spica.psf: PSF (protein structure file) containing system topology

  • toppar/enm_bond_index.ndx: ENM bond index file (only when -Go option is used)

  • toppar/enm_bond_parm.prm: ENM bond parameter file (only when -Go option is used)

  • toppar/enm_angle_index.ndx: ENM angle index file (only when -Go option is used)

  • toppar/enm_angle_parm.prm: ENM angle parameter file (only when -Go option is used)

The RTF and parameter files in the toppar/ directory and the PSF file are required to run GENESIS CG-MD with SPICA FF. Use these files with the control file generated by gen_gnsin to start the simulation. For systems with Go model proteins (-Go option), ENM files are automatically generated and should be included in the GENESIS control file.

Example¶

Lipid membrane system (no proteins):

cg_spica setup_gns DOPC.top 128 WAT.top 1408 spica_db.prm dopc.cg.pdb

DOPC.top WAT.top spica_db.prm dopc.cg.pdb

Output files:

toppar/top_spica_charmm.rtf toppar/par_spica_charmm.prm gns_spica.psf

Protein with Go model or ENM:

cg_spica setup_gns -Go protein.top.v2.Go 1 WAT.top 5000 spica_db.prm protein.cg.pdb

The -Go option treats each protein as an independent topology. This generates ENM files in addition to the standard output files:

  • toppar/enm_bond_index.ndx

  • toppar/enm_bond_parm.prm

  • toppar/enm_angle_index.ndx

  • toppar/enm_angle_parm.prm

Multiple protein copies with Go/ENM:

cg_spica setup_gns -Go protein.top.v2.Go 3 WAT.top 5000 spica_db.prm system.pdb

With -Go, the 3 protein copies are treated as 3 independent topology entries (each with nmol=1), ensuring correct Go/ENM interaction indexing for each molecule.

Positional args¶

topfile <topology>

topology file of a molecule

nmol <int>

number of molecules

paramfile <parameter>

SPICA force field parameter file

coordfile <.pdb>

CG configuration file

Optional args¶

-Go

Enable special handling for proteins with Go model or ENM. When this option is used, each protein molecule is treated as an independent topology entry, even when multiple copies (nmol > 1) are specified. This is required for correct indexing of Go/ENM interactions within each protein molecule.

Important Notes¶

File Order

The order of topology files and molecule numbers must match the order of molecules in the PDB file.

Multiple Molecules

When specifying multiple molecule types, provide them in pairs: topfile1 nmol1 topfile2 nmol2 ... paramfile pdbfile

When to Use -Go Option

Use -Go when your topology file was generated by the Go or ENM commands and contains proteins with Go-like model or elastic network constraints. This ensures that each protein copy is treated independently for correct interaction indexing.

Example with 3 protein copies:

  • Without -Go: Creates 1 topology with nmol=3

  • With -Go: Creates 3 independent topologies, each with nmol=1

ENM File Detection

When topology files contain ENM or Go model parameters, the program automatically generates ENM parameter files (enm_bond_index.ndx, enm_bond_parm.prm, enm_angle_index.ndx, enm_angle_parm.prm) in the toppar/ directory. These files are automatically detected by gen_gnsin.

Complete Workflow¶

Step 1: Generate topology and parameter files

cg_spica setup_gns DOPC.top 128 WAT.top 1408 spica_db.prm system.pdb

Step 2: Generate GENESIS control file

cg_spica gen_gnsin -pdb system.pdb

Step 3: Run GENESIS simulation

export OMP_NUM_THREADS=4
mpirun -n 8 spdyn npt.inp > npt.log

See Also¶

  • gen_gnsin - Generate GENESIS input files

  • setup_lmp - Similar tool for LAMMPS

  • setup_gmx - Similar tool for GROMACS

  • ENM - Generate elastic network topology

  • Go - Generate Go model topology

  • GENESIS - MD simulation software
